_provenance.method: CPU vs GPU parity validation for barracuda BatchedEt0, BatchedWaterBalance, BatchedRichards
_provenance._tolerance_justification: Bit-identical parity requires 1e-10; batch scaling and metalForge routing are deterministic.

experiment: Exp 040 — CPU vs GPU Parity Validation
description: Validates that barracuda GPU dispatch (BatchedEt0, BatchedWaterBalance, BatchedRichards) produces identical results to the validated CPU path. Tests the core claim: pure Rust math is portable across compute substrates. When GPU hardware is unavailable, the GPU API falls back to CPU — we validate that this fallback path produces bit-identical results to the direct CPU API.
references: Allen et al. (1998) FAO-56 — ET₀ reference implementation

et0_cpu_gpu_parity.description: BatchedEt0 CPU path vs direct daily_et0 — must be bit-identical

water_balance_cpu_gpu_parity.description: BatchedWaterBalance GPU step vs CPU step — must match

batch_scaling.description: CPU batch produces consistent results regardless of batch size
batch_scaling.note: Every element in the batch must be identical to a single-element computation

metalforge_routing.description: metalForge correctly routes workloads to available substrates
checks: et0_batch routes to GPU when available
crop_stress_classifier routes to NPU when available
fallback to CPU when preferred substrate unavailable
